description: Command injection via shell_exec with unsanitized GET parameter

expected.notes: $_GET['cmd'] flows directly into shell_exec without sanitization
shell_exec output assigned to $output and echoed — tainted shell output flows to echo (XSS-style response sink)

escapeshellarg sanitizes CMDI but not XSS; tainted shell output still flows to echo. Scanner produces this XSS-like flow; aspirationally this secondary flow might be clamped if XSS models recognized shell_exec-of-sanitized-input as non-propagating
TP: AST pattern match on shell_exec($cmd) at line 3; $_GET['cmd'] flows unsanitized into shell_exec
escapeshellarg sanitizes input at line 7 — php.cmdi.system must NOT fire on line 8 (regression guard, Layer B suppression)
